Actúa como un experto en Dashboarding y JavaScript.

Tarea: Crear un Dashboard "Full Screen" para FarmaPlus que consuma datos de un Google Sheets publicado.

1. Configuración de Fuente de Datos:

URL: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vRkCTRPfVd-JDs_ALNrq3JxHsl6hGQR90Vs243vt1iGo8vVhVhnkSvgbC4DQ9R-Vw/pubhtml?gid=584708407&single=true

Estructura de la Tabla: > - Encabezados: [ASESOR, PEDIDOS, Monto 10:00, Monto 11:00, ..., Monto 19:00]

Fila 18: Contiene los TOTALES globales.

Celda B41: Contiene el Monto Objetivo Diario.

2. Lógica de Actualización Automática:

Implementa un setInterval en JavaScript para que la función de "fetch" de datos se ejecute cada 5 minutos (300000 ms) sin recargar toda la página, solo actualizando los gráficos y números.

3. Diseño Visual (Dark Mode Moderno):

Colores HEX: Azul: #325795, Rojo: #C8102E, Verde Éxito: #01B693, Gris: #F5F5F5. (No usar "Verde Modo").

Layout Full Screen: Usa overflow: hidden en el body. Divide la pantalla en:

Top: KPIs gigantes (Venta Total, Pedidos, % de Objetivo).

Centro: Lado izquierdo Ranking de Asesores (Bar Chart); Lado derecho Evolución Horaria (Line Chart).

Logo: Ubicar en la esquina inferior derecha. URL: https://ugc.production.linktr.ee/5ef65f71-15a5-4bc2-8e40-71e49e3483e5_FarmaPlus-ORIGINAL-H.png

4. Requerimiento Técnico de Código:

Usa un solo archivo HTML.

Utiliza Tailwind CSS vía CDN y Chart.js.

IMPORTANTE: Para leer el Google Sheets, usa la técnica de fetch a la URL y parsea el DOM de la tabla HTML resultante buscando la tr número 18 para totales y la lógica de filas para asesores.

Formatea moneda: $#,###.00.

Genera el código optimizado y listo para usar.

**Defining the Objective**

**Structuring the Project**

I've been mapping out the project's structure, focusing on component design and the data flow. The architecture is taking shape: `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and a dedicated `components/` directory. Next, I'm thinking about the layout: KPIs at the top, a ranking bar chart on the left, an evolution line chart on the right, and the logo at the bottom. The goal is to make it a full-screen, dark-mode dashboard.


**Refining the Data Handling**

I'm now diving deep into data handling, focusing on the `services/` directory. I'm building out the data fetching and parsing logic. The user specifically mentioned the data is coming from a published HTML view of a Google Sheet, which presents a CORS challenge. To ensure a smooth process for the user, I'll leverage a CORS proxy. My goal is to ensure data parsing works out of the box. I will also incorporate `types.ts` for definitions and `constants.ts` for color and configurations.


**Securing Data Access**

Okay, the CORS proxy is now integrated. I'm leveraging `https://api.allorigins.win/get?url=${encodedURL}` to handle cross-origin requests. My attention is now on the data parsing logic. I'm preparing to use `DOMParser` to extract information from the HTML. Specifically, I'm targeting the table, the totals row (around index 17), cell B41 (the target), and the advisor data.


**Refining Visual Aesthetics**

I'm now zeroing in on the visual styling. The plan is to create a dark theme using Zinc/Slate 900 for the background and a grey color, which the prompt specifies as #F5F5F5, for the text. Accents will be blue, red, and green, as per the design request.

\n\n
Okay, I'm working through the implementation steps. First is setting up the metadata and `DashboardData` interface. Next, I'm crafting `sheetService.ts` for data fetching using a proxy and `DOMParser`. The parsing logic is focusing on identifying the table and extracting specific rows and cells. I'll target Row 18 for totals (index 17/18, potentially), and cell B41. I will have to iterate for data rows to extract name, orders, and hourly segments.


**Developing Component Logic**

I'm now focusing on component structure and application logic. I'm going to create `KpiCard` as a simple pure component, an `AdvisorChart` for the horizontal bar chart, and an `HourlyChart` for the line chart. In `App.tsx`, I'll use `useEffect` for initial loading and `setInterval` for updating. I need state management for data, loading states, and error handling. I need to handle currency parsing, specifically using robust number parsing to account for potential locale variations.


**Enhancing Visuals & Charts**

I'm now focusing on the visual styling for the charts. I'm setting up dark mode configuration for the chart.js components, including grid lines and text colors. I'm also ensuring chart.js components are registered. This includes `CategoryScale`, `LinearScale`, `BarElement`, `PointElement`, `LineElement`, `Title`, `Tooltip`, and `Legend`. I will be including colors such as Blue `#325795`, Red `#C8102E`, Green `#01B693`, and Grey `#F5F5F5` to create an elegant and informative display.


**Improving Data Accuracy**

I'm now refining the data parsing, focusing on row selection. I am now aware that "Row 18" may be offset by headers. I'll include error handling in case the row isn't found, but I am going to trust the data source. I'm also ensuring the table structure aligns with the columns of advisor data. I will focus on extracting names, orders, and hourly segments.


**Finalizing Currency Display**

I'm now focusing on formatting currency with `Intl.NumberFormat`, targeting the `$#,###.00` pattern for clarity. I'm preparing to use `chart.js`, `react-chartjs-2`, and `lucide-react` for icon inclusion as core dependencies. I have reviewed all constraints, confirming React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind, single HTML file, fetch, 5-minute refresh, and specific colors. Finally, the project structure is finalized.


**Confirming Table Indices**

Okay, I'm verifying the table indexing now. To clarify, row 18 translates to index 17, row 41 is index 40, and column B corresponds to index 1. With these index offsets confirmed, I'm ready to proceed with data extraction.
